item.title: ★ European Commission: ‘Guidance to Google for AI Interoperability on Android & Sharing of Google Search’ 摘要
item.description:
The European Commission issued binding specification measures to Google under the Digital Markets Act, requiring Google to give third-party AI assistants equal access to Android device features (hardware buttons, screen capture, sensors, background operation, hot word detection) and share Google Search user data with competitors. The author criticizes the scope as potentially turning Android into a PC-like mess, risking privacy violations and battery drain. Possible outcomes include Google wasting resources on unused APIs, major privacy scandals, or Google downgrading Gemini in the EU. The author argues the EC is overreaching into OS design, with likely negative consequences for users and innovation.
标签: EU, Digital Markets Act, Google, Android, AI assistants, privacy, regulation, competition, search data, operating system
